2605.07897.title: Semantic-Aware Adaptive Visual Memory for Streaming Video Understanding
2605.07897.paper_summary: Online streaming video understanding requires models to process continuous visual inputs and respond to user queries in real time, where the unbounded stream and unpredictable query timing turn memory management into a central challenge. Existing methods typically compress visual tokens via visual similarity heuristics, or augment compression with KV-cache-level retrieval. However, compression decisions rarely incorporate semantic signals, and retrieval is often added after compression is finalized, making the two stages hard to coordinate. We present SAVEMem, a training-free dual-stage framework that brings semantic awareness into memory generation and lets the retrieval scope adapt per query. In Stage~1, SAVEMem builds a three-tier streaming memory online under a constant memory budget. A fixed pseudo-question bank provides a lightweight semantic prior, so that long-term retention is shaped by semantic salience rather than visual similarity alone. In Stage~2, SAVEMem performs query-aware retrieval over this memory. An anchor-conditioned recency gate adapts the retrieval scope from short-term to mid- and long-term memory based on whether the query targets the present or the distant past. Within this scope, late interaction between query and memory tokens selects candidate frames for answering. Applied to Qwen2.5-VL without training, SAVEMem improves the OVO-Bench overall score from 52.27 to 62.69 and yields consistent gains on StreamingBench and ODV-Bench, while reducing peak GPU memory by 48\% at 128 frames over the backbone.
2605.07897.paper_summary_zh: 在線串流視頻理解需要模型處理連續的視覺輸入並即時響應用戶查詢，其中無界流和不可預測的查詢時機使得記憶管理成為一個核心挑戰。現有的方法通常通過視覺相似性啟發式壓縮視覺標記，或通過KV-cache級別檢索增強壓縮。然而，壓縮決策很少納入語義信號，且檢索通常是在壓縮完成後添加的，這使得兩個階段難以協調。我們提出了SAVEMem，一種無需訓練的雙階段框架，將語義意識引入記憶生成，並讓檢索範圍根據查詢進行調整。在第一階段，SAVEMem在固定的記憶預算下在線構建三層串流記憶。一個固定的偽問題庫提供輕量級的語義先驗，使得長期保留受到語義顯著性而非僅僅是視覺相似性的影響。在第二階段，SAVEMem對這個記憶進行查詢感知檢索。一個基於錨點的近期門根據查詢是針對當前還是遙遠過去，調整檢索範圍從短期到中期和長期記憶。在這個範圍內，查詢和記憶標記之間的後期互動選擇候選幀以進行回答。應用於Qwen2.5-VL而無需訓練，SAVEMem將OVO-Bench的總分從52.27提高到62.69，並在StreamingBench和ODV-Bench上產生一致的增益，同時在128幀的基礎上將峰值GPU記憶減少了48%。
2605.07897.author: Hang Wu et.al.
2605.07897.authors: Hang Wu, Sherin Mary Mathews, Yujun Cai, Ming-Hsuan Yang, Yiwei Wang
